steps.title: Read the graph at 200 kelvins
steps.content: The question asks for the estimated volume when the temperature is $200$ kelvins. Locate $x=200$ on the horizontal axis, then move to the graph and read the corresponding $y$-value.

steps.title: Read the volume
steps.content: At $x=200$, the graph shows $y=9$. Since $y$ is measured in liters, the estimated volume is $9$ liters.

steps.title: Confirm the answer
steps.content: The estimated volume at $200$ kelvins is $9$ liters, which matches choice B.
